text: A sensitivity family $\{\xi_s\}_{s \in S}$ satisfies \emph{Iverson's law of similarity} if there exist continuous functions $\gamma\colon J \times S \to \R \setminus \{0\}$ and $\eta\colon J \times S \to S$ with the boundary conditions $\gamma(1, s) = 1$ and $\eta(1, s) = s$.
Suppose $\xi$ satisfies Iverson's law of similarity with $\eta(\lambda, s) = f(s)$ for a continuous function $f\colon S \to S$.
asserts: f is continuous function S → S
notes: PRE-FIX state: hypothesis allows arbitrary f. Post-#60 fix this prop also explicitly notes 'f = id forced by boundary'.

text: Case (B) Logarithmic: $\gamma(\lambda, s) = \lambda^b\, e^{c(s - f(s))/a}$ (reduces to $\lambda^b$ at $f = \mathrm{id}$; $f$ may be any continuous map here).
asserts: Case B: gamma(lambda, s) = lambda^b * e^{c(s - f(s))/a}
Reduces to lambda^b at f = id
f may be any continuous map here
notes: PRE-FIX state. Post-#60 fix: 'f may be any' language deleted, gamma simplified to lambda^b.

text: Case (C) Power: $\gamma(\lambda, s) = \lambda^b\, \sqrt[a]{(s + c_2/c)/(f(s) + c_2/c)}$ (reduces to $\lambda^b$ at $f = \mathrm{id}$; $f$ may be any continuous map here).
asserts: Case C: gamma(lambda, s) involves radical with f(s)
f may be any continuous map here
